result.title: Profile
result.description:
Many connectors (especially OAuth-based) act on behalf of a user.
This class represents the user's profile, which is typically retrieved from
the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

Implementation details for different connectors vary, so we can't guarantee
that all fields will be present.

tool.name: linear (legacy)_search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search Linear issues. The ``filters`` dictionary accepts the following keys: ``user_id`` (call ``get_profile`` to obtain the ID of the current user), ``assigned_to_me`` (boolean), ``assignee_name``, ``assignee_id``, ``creator_name``, ``creator_id``, ``project_name``, ``project_id``, ``priority``(integer), ``team_id``, ``team_name``, ``state_id``, ``state_name``, and date fields: ``createdAt``,``updatedAt``, ``completedAt``, ``startedAt`` using ``[op, value]`` where ``op`` is one of ``eq``, ``neq``, ``lt``, ``lte``, ``gt``, ``gte``. Example: ``{"user_id": "usr_123", "project_name": "Backend", "createdAt": ["gte", "2025-01-01"]}``. ``query`` is a free-text search query. ``recency_days`` is ignored. ``topn`` is the number of results to return. If ``query`` is empty, the most recently updated issues are returned.

query.description: Free-text search query used to match issues, projects, and teams.
query.title: Query

topn.description: Maximum number of issues to return.
topn.title: Topn

recency_days.description: Unused compatibility parameter. Linear search ignores recency hints.
recency_days.title: Recency Days

filters.description: Structured Linear filters such as assignee, creator, project, team, state, priority, and date comparators.
filters.title: Filters

connector_scopes.description: Optional Linear scope hints encoded as canonical_handle -> display_name mappings.
connector_scopes.title: Connector Scopes

tool.name: linear (legacy)_search_issues
tool.title: search_issues
tool.description: Search issues by free-text query against title/description. Optionally restrict to open issues only. Returns a page of matching issues and pagination info. If ``query`` is empty, the most recently updated issues are returned. DO NOT USE THIS WITH call_api. For comprehensive filterable search use `search` instead.

open_only.description: Whether to restrict results to open issues only.
open_only.title: Open Only

page_size.description: Maximum number of issues to return.
page_size.title: Page Size

page_cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous result page.
page_cursor.title: Page Cursor

tools.namespace_description: Find and reference issues and projects.

tool.name: linear (legacy)_search_issues_generic_filter
tool.title: search_issues_generic_filter
tool.description: Search Linear issues using a raw GraphQL filter.

issue_filter.description: Raw Linear GraphQL filter object to apply. For supported fields and comparator semantics, see Linear's GraphQL docs at https://studio.apollographql.com/public/Linear-API/variant/current/schema/reference/inputs/IssueFilter and https://linear.app/developers/filtering. Typical shape: nested objects such as {'title': {'containsIgnoreCase': 'bug'}}, {'team': {'key': {'eq': 'ENG'}}}, {'project': {'id': {'eq': '<project-id>'}}}, {'state': {'name': {'eq': 'In Progress'}}}, {'completedAt': {'null': True}}, or {'and': [{'priority': {'lte': 2}}, {'assignee': {'email': {'eq': 'alice@example.com'}}}]}. Common top-level fields include title, description, team, project, state, labels, assignee, creator, priority, estimate, createdAt, updatedAt, startedAt, completedAt, and, or, parent, and children. This filter is passed through directly to Linear, so unsupported keys or invalid comparators will be rejected by Linear.
issue_filter.title: Issue Filter

page_size.description: Maximum number of issues to return.
page_size.title: Page Size

page_cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous result page.
page_cursor.title: Page Cursor
